title: Harness Enforcement Manifest (v1)
description: Canonical machine-readable inventory of every enforcement/doctrine unit in the harness (NL Overhaul task C.1, ADR 058 D3). One entry per unit: a unit is either a doctrine surface (compact under adapters/claude-code/doctrine/, or rules/constitution.md) with its enforcing hooks, or a hook-only unit (doctrine_file null). Coverage contract enforced by scripts/manifest-check.sh: every *.sh under adapters/claude-code/hooks/ (excluding lib/; attic/ is a sibling dir and never scanned) appears in at least one entry's hooks[], and every doctrine target from the Wave C disposition table (specs-c §C.0) appears as some entry's doctrine_file. Consumed by hooks/harness-doctor.sh (manifest-driven claim-honesty), hooks/doctrine-jit.sh (jit_triggers path matching, task C.2), and scripts/manifest-check.sh --gen-index (generated doctrine/INDEX.md). Schema version 1 (initial).

schema_version.description: Schema version. Currently always 1. Validators accept current + N-1 versions for graceful migration.

entries.description: One entry per enforcement/doctrine unit, sorted by id (LC_ALL=C order) for deterministic diffs and --gen-index output.

id.description: Kebab-case slug uniquely identifying the unit within the manifest.

kind.description: gate = can refuse/block an action or session (or is a warn-mode gate — see blocking); writer = emits state/artifacts as a side effect, never blocks; surfacer = read-only informational output at a lifecycle boundary; pattern = self-applied discipline with no hook; convention = per-surface coding convention with no hook.

doctrine_file.description: Path (relative to adapters/claude-code/) of the doctrine compact this unit's substance lives in: doctrine/<name>.md, rules/constitution.md, or null for hook-only units with no doctrine surface. During the doctrine buildout (before the generated doctrine/INDEX.md exists — written by manifest-check.sh --gen-index at C.5) manifest-check.sh WARNs (not REDs) on missing doctrine/ targets; once the INDEX exists the check is fully RED-enforcing.

hooks.description: Basenames of hook scripts under adapters/claude-code/hooks/ implementing this unit. Empty for pattern/convention units. Each disk hook must appear in exactly one entry (coverage checked both ways by manifest-check.sh).

events.description: Lifecycle events the unit's hooks fire on. Claude Code hook events use their canonical casing; precommit/prepush denote git-hook dispatch (pre-commit-gate.sh chain or git-hooks/pre-push); manual denotes on-demand invocation. Empty for pattern/convention units.

wired_template.description: True iff EVERY basename in hooks[] appears in adapters/claude-code/settings.json.template (derived by parsing the template; inline compound-command references count). False when hooks is empty (hook-derived field; no hooks means nothing is wired) or when any hook is absent from the template — git-hook-dispatched and chain-invoked hooks are the honest false cases, named in honest_status.

selftest.description: True iff EVERY hook in hooks[] contains a --self-test entrypoint (grep-derived). False when hooks is empty or when any hook lacks one.

jit_triggers.description: Just-in-time doctrine-injection triggers consumed by hooks/doctrine-jit.sh (task C.2). paths come from the specs-c §C.0 JIT column; keywords is reserved and empty in v1.

paths.description: Path substrings/globs; an Edit/Write whose file_path matches any of them triggers a once-per-session injection of this unit's doctrine compact.

keywords.description: Reserved for v2 keyword triggers. Empty array in v1.

blocking.description: True iff the unit can refuse an action or block session end in its default mode. Warn-mode-default gates (e.g. doc-gate, principles-compliance) and PostToolUse feedback hooks are blocking: false.

honest_status.description: REQUIRED (non-empty) when kind is 'gate' AND wired_template is false: names honestly how/where the gate actually fires (git-hook dispatcher, pre-commit chain, sub-invocation) or which Wave lands its wiring (e.g. 'pending Wave D'). Omit or null otherwise. Enforced by manifest-check.sh check (e) and the conditional below.

budget_class.description: The single settings.json chain this unit is budgeted against for the Wave F chain-length budgets (Stop <= 6, SessionStart <= 8, blocking <= 12). 'none' for git-hook-dispatched, manual, multi-chain, and hook-less units.

waiver_path.description: Path/glob (relative to ~/.claude/state/ or the repo) of the structured waiver file this blocking gate honors, typically validated by hooks/lib/waiver-purpose-clause.sh. Null or omitted when honesty_rationale explains why no waiver is offered.

honesty_rationale.description: REQUIRED (non-null) when blocking is true AND waiver_path is null/absent: names why this gate's block is session-honesty-class (resolvable by the session that created the gap, e.g. fix the commit content) rather than a world-state assertion needing a waiver, per ADR 059 D4 scoping. Enforced by manifest-check.sh's waiver-parity check.

added_after.description: OPTIONAL YYYY-MM string marking this entry as subject to the new-gate-evidence-bar (ADR 059 D4): entries with added_after >= '2026-07' (lexicographic) must also carry golden_scenario, fp_expectation, retirement_condition, and (waiver_path OR honesty_rationale). Omitted/null for pre-existing entries not subject to the bar.

golden_scenario.description: OPTIONAL (required by the new-gate-evidence-bar when added_after >= '2026-07'): names the concrete real-world scenario this gate/check was built to catch.

fp_expectation.description: OPTIONAL (required by the new-gate-evidence-bar when added_after >= '2026-07'): the expected false-positive rate/profile, naming what a legitimate session doing this looks like so it is distinguishable from the caught scenario.

retirement_condition.description: OPTIONAL (required by the new-gate-evidence-bar when added_after >= '2026-07'): the condition under which this gate/check should be retired or demoted (ADR 059 D7 gate-demotion.sh reads this class of signal).
